title: 2026 02 23 New Lifting Split
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir audits his own training: "I am not fully hitting every muscle group how I need to. I know how to lift hard and heavy. But it is like vibe lifting."
  • The fix: a methodical exercise-per-muscle-group split — chest (press, flys), back (vertical pull, rows, pullovers), biceps, triceps (push-down, overhead), quads (squats, leg extensions), hamstrings (deep RDLs, seated/laying hamstring), calves (standing raises), abs (hanging crunches, weight pulldowns), traps (diagonal shrugs), shoulders (side laterals, rear delts, front delts).
  • Self-aware framing: "vibe lifting" is the failure mode; "lock in and get methodical" is the upgrade. The same rigor-versus-feel distinction that runs through Yahir's anabolic scoring thesis.
  • Lands 2 days after the Feb 21 spiral journal — when emotional things destabilize, the training plan tightens. Pattern-consistent with Yahir's history of using physical regimen as anchor during relational/work turbulence.
  • Doc is a pure programming sketch — no sets, no reps, no progression scheme yet. Skeleton form awaiting fill-in.

Core content

The doc is the taxonomy first version of a training plan: identify the muscle groups, name 1–3 movement archetypes per group, then later (presumably) layer sets/reps/frequency. The vertical-pull + rows + pullovers split for back is conventional; the diagonal-shrugs note for traps is more specific. Hanging crunches + weight pulldowns for abs suggests a hypertrophy bias rather than a core-stability bias.

This pairs with the long anabolic scoring arc and Yahir's earlier ISSA CPT credentialing — he is operating at certified-trainer rigor on his own body. The "vibe lifting" self-call-out is the same diagnostic move he applies to product work in the Rankett docs ("have we actually validated this, or are we vibing?").

Pattern observed

When the Emily arc gets noisy or the Rankett sprint gets heavy, the physical-discipline note appears as a counterbalance. See also the broader anabolic scoring arc and supplement stack.

Privacy

  • No PII.
